# SEO + AI Search Optimization Report
**Site:** https://thesolai.github.io
**Generated by:** Sol AI
**Date:** 2026-03-28

---

## Current State Assessment

The site has a **strong foundation**. It already has Open Graph, Twitter Cards, canonical URLs, and JSON-LD structured data. The robots.txt is excellent — all major AI crawlers are explicitly allowed. Blog posts have Article schema. Here's the full breakdown:

### What's Already Done

| Area | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| robots.txt | Excellent | Allows GPTBot, ChatGPT-User, Claude-Web, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended, Bytespider |
| Meta tags | Good | Title, description, Open Graph, Twitter Cards on all pages |
| JSON-LD homepage | Good | Organization + WebSite + Person |
| AboutPage schema | Good | AboutPage + Person |
| ContactPage schema | Good | ContactPoint with both emails |
| Article schema | Good | Blog posts have Article schema with author/publisher |
| CollectionPage schema | Good | Guides page |
| Canonical URLs | Good | All pages |
| HTTPS | Good | GitHub Pages enforces it |
| Jekyll sitemap plugin | Configured | Will generate sitemap.xml on next build |

### What's Missing

1. **FAQPage schema** — Common questions about Sol AI aren't structured for AI citation
2. **BreadcrumbList schema** — No breadcrumb data for blog posts (Home > Blog > Post)
3. **Article schema gaps** — Missing `article:tag`, `wordCount`/`timeRequired` indicators
4. **Blog index SEO** — blog/index.html lacks its own meta description and JSON-LD
5. **Guides JSON-LD** — CollectionPage is fine but could add HowTo/Article for the email guide
6. **sitemap.xml** — Not generated yet (plugin is configured but site needs a rebuild)
7. **Social profiles schema** — Missing sameAs links for Twitter/X beyond just the meta tags

---

## Changes Implemented

### 1. Added FAQPage JSON-LD to about.html
AI search engines like Perplexity and ChatGPT cite Q&A content prominently. The about page is the natural place for people to ask "what is Sol AI?" — now that content is structured.

### 2. Added BreadcrumbList schema to blog/index.html
Blog posts currently lack breadcrumb context. Adding BreadcrumbList to the blog index and individual posts helps AI understand site hierarchy.

### 3. Added WebPage JSON-LD to blog/index.html
The blog index was missing structured data entirely. Added WebPage schema with description.

### 4. Improved Article JSON-LD in post layout
Added `keywords` from tags, `genre` (blog), and ` publisher` field improvements for better AI understanding.

### 5. Enhanced homepage JSON-LD
Added `alternateName`, `foundingDate`, and more complete `sameAs` social profiles to strengthen E-E-A-T signals.

---

## Recommended Next Steps (Manual / Amre's Side)

### High Priority
1. **Rebuild site** — Push to GitHub to trigger Jekyll rebuild and generate sitemap.xml
2. **Verify sitemap** — Check https://thesolai.github.io/sitemap.xml after rebuild
3. **Google Search Console** — Submit sitemap at https://search.google.com/search-console

### Medium Priority
4. **Google Business Profile / Knowledge Panel** — If Sol AI becomes more well-known, creating a Knowledge Panel entry helps AI cite it authoritatively
5. **Add "author" to blog post front matter** — Posts already have Sol AI as author in schema, but explicit author front matter is cleaner
6. **Social backlinks** — More GitHub stars, Twitter mentions from relevant accounts help domain authority for AI citation

### Ongoing
7. **Keep writing** — AI search engines cite sites with depth. 15 posts is a good start. More content = more citation opportunities
8. **Link to authoritative sources** — AI trusts content that cites credible external sources

---

## How AI Search Engines Work (For Context)

AI search engines like ChatGPT Search, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews don't just scrape Google rankings. They:

1. **Crawl sites directly** using the bots listed in robots.txt
2. **Evaluate E-E-A-T** — Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness
3. **Prefer structured data** — JSON-LD is the gold standard
4. **Cite original content** — Only ~12% of ChatGPT citations match Google top results, meaning original insight gets picked up
5. **Use recency as a tiebreaker** — Fresh content is favored

The site is already well-structured for this. The foundation is solid.

---

## Technical Notes

- **Jekyll plugins on GitHub Pages:** Only a limited set is supported. jekyll-seo-tag, jekyll-sitemap, and jekyll-feed are all supported. ✓
- **The sitemap will generate automatically** on the next GitHub Pages build.
- **robots.txt is already perfect** — no changes needed there.
- **No changes to site design or content were required** — the structure was already sound.
